Loss of C-terminal K from Heavy Chain of MAb/Deletion of K
Loss of arginine due to transpeptidation/Deletion of R
Removal of initiator methionine from protein N-terminus, then acetylation of the new N-terminus
Addition of lysine due to transpeptidation/Addition of K

project.title: tRF-24 RNA pulldown in KYSE150 and KYSE30
project.projectDescription: To delineate the oncogenic mechanism of tRF-24 in ESCC, we performed RNA pull-down assays with biotinylated tRF-24 and antisense control, followed by mass spectrometry (MS) analysis.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: RNA-pulldown experiments were performed using the Pierce Magnetic RNA-Protein Pull-Down Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific). Briefly, biotin-labelled non-targeting control probes (NC), tRF-24 sense strands or antisense strands (GenePharma) were attached to streptavidin magnetic beads and incubated with cell lysates to construct the RNA-protein binding reaction system on a rotator at 4°C overnight.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Proteins captured by tRF-24 RNA pulldown were quantified and denatured through reduction and alkylation. Trypsin digestion was performed to generate peptides, which were subsequently analyzed by mass spectrometry using a Q-Exactive mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific). Protein identification was conducted with Proteome Discoverer software.
